gaps.reason: stated exposure has no matching coverage type in the policy schedule
exposure peril is named in a policy exclusion with no buy-back endorsement
stated exposure value exceeds the matching coverage limit
stated exposure value exceeds the applicable category sublimit
deductible exceeds 10% of the stated exposure value
a recommended endorsement for a stated exposure is not attached to the policy

not_evaluable.why: no coverage declares a coinsurance clause

review_prompts: Stated exposure values are client-provided and unverified; confirm replacement cost vs. actual-cash-value basis.
Other policies (umbrella, standalone/NFIP flood, scheduled personal-articles, auto) may already cover the exposure.
A high deductible or a lower limit may be an intentional premium trade-off the client chose.
Blanket, agreed-value, or extended-replacement-cost provisions may apply instead of scheduled limits.
Statutory minimums, lender, or lease requirements may govern the required limits in this jurisdiction.
disclaimer: Coverage-gap analysis only; not a coverage, eligibility, or claim determination and not insurance or legal advice. Consult a licensed insurance professional before acting.
narrative: Coverage-gap analysis of policy HO****-1234 (HO-3) against 5 stated exposures for profile CGA-****4471 as of 2026-07-15 surfaced 6 evaluable gaps. The dwelling replacement exposure (450,000) is above the Coverage A limit (300,000), a 150,000 shortfall. The flood exposure has no matching coverage in the schedule and flood is a named exclusion with no buy-back endorsement. The scheduled jewelry value (15,000) is above the personal-property jewelry sublimit (1,500). The home-business equipment names a recommended home_business endorsement that is not attached. The water-backup deductible (2,500) is 31% of that exposure value. Coinsurance could not be evaluated because no coverage declares a coinsurance clause. Suggested review priority: Elevated. These are questions for a licensed insurance professional to weigh against the review prompts, not conclusions. Coverage-gap analysis only; not a coverage, eligibility, or claim determination and not insurance or legal advice. Consult a licensed insurance professional before acting.
